hypothesis: Integrating a dedicated molecular image detection and segmentation pre-processing module—trained specifically to localize and isolate chemical structure depictions within heterogeneous scientific article layouts—into the single-agent pipeline will yield a statistically significant improvement in SMILES extraction F1 scores (from near-zero to above 0.40) across both nanomaterial and small molecule datasets in ChemX, as measured against the ground-truth SMILES annotations, because the current near-zero performance is attributable specifically to the absence of reliable molecular image localization rather than to the downstream SMILES generation capability of the language model.
